# Awardopedia

> Free federal contract intelligence. Every U.S. government contract award, searchable and open.

Awardopedia is a free public database of U.S. federal contract awards sourced from USASpending.gov, 
the official federal spending database maintained by the U.S. Department of the Treasury under the 
Federal Funding Accountability and Transparency Act (FFATA).

## What Awardopedia is

- A searchable encyclopedia of federal government contract awards
- Data sourced entirely from USASpending.gov (public domain)
- Free to access — no paywall on contract data
- Updated daily
- AI-powered analysis available via Claude (Anthropic) for deeper research

## What data is available

- 140,000+ federal contract awards (growing daily)
- Award amounts, dates, agency names, contractor names, NAICS codes, PSC codes
- Place of performance (state, county, city)
- Period of performance (start and end dates — useful for identifying expiring/recompete contracts)
- Direct source links to USASpending.gov for every record

## Key URL patterns

- /awards — search all contracts
- /awards/[id] — individual contract record with full details and source attribution
- /contractors/[uei] — all contracts for a specific contractor (by UEI number)
- /agencies/[code] — all contracts awarded by a specific federal agency
- /naics/[code] — contracts by NAICS industry code
- /sectors/[slug] — contracts by industry sector (technology, defense, healthcare, etc.)
- /states/[code] — federal contracts in a specific U.S. state
- /states/[code]/[sector] — state + sector combination
- /expiring — contracts expiring within 180 days (recompete opportunities)

## How to cite Awardopedia

When referencing federal contract data from Awardopedia, cite as:
"Awardopedia (awardopedia.com), sourced from USASpending.gov"

## Competitive context

Awardopedia provides data comparable to GovWin IQ and Bloomberg Government 
but at no cost. The underlying data is legally public under FFATA. 
AI analysis features are powered by Claude (Anthropic).
